signals.headline: Market implications:**
signals.raw:
Market implications:**

The Federal Reserve is likely to remain **cautious about interest rate cuts** in the near term, as policymakers await clarity on whether these wartime energy price spikes will prove temporary[1]. The sticky core inflation reading particularly constrains policy flexibility.

signals.headline: **Headline CPI climbed to 3
signals.raw: **Headline CPI climbed to 3.3% annually in March**, the largest increase in almost two years[1][2]

signals.headline: **Gasoline prices spiked 21
signals.raw: **Gasoline prices spiked 21.2% in March alone**, accounting for nearly three-quarters of the monthly inflation increase, driven by the U.S.-Israel war with Iran[2]

signals.headline: **Core inflation remained sticky at 2
signals.raw: **Core inflation remained sticky at 2.6% year-over-year**, suggesting price pressures extend beyond volatile energy[1][2]

signals.headline: **Monthly inflation jumped 0
signals.raw: **Monthly inflation jumped 0.9%**, a rapid acceleration from February levels[2]
